Jitendra Malik: Building AI from the ground-up, sensorimotor before language

Podcast Episode # Jitendra Malik: Building AI from the ground-up, sensorimotor before language The Robot Brains Podcast

Aug 17, 2023

1 hr 15 min

## Episode Description

[Jitendra Malik](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=aOklxsQAAAAJ&hl=en) , [Professor of EECS](https://www2.eecs.berkeley.edu/Faculty/Homepages/malik.html) at UC Berkeley discusses with host [P](https://people.eecs.berkeley.edu/~pabbeel/)

… Show more

## More episodes like this

* * *

### Degrees of Change: How Quantum Could Transform AI Infrastructure

Paul, Weiss Waking Up With AI

In this episode, Katherine Forrest and Scott Caravello unpack how quantum computing could amplify AI's capabilities while transforming the data centers that power it, and why the road from research lab to real-world impact is both closer—and more complicated—than you might think. ## Learn More About Paul, Weiss’s Artificial Intelligence practice: https://www.paulweiss.com/industries/artificial-intelligence

Mar 26

23 min 45 sec

* * *

### Chatting With a Chatbot: The History of the First Clinical Chatbots, Straight From an LLM

JAMA+ AI Conversations

A large language model (LLM) details the history of 2 early chatbots, ELIZA and PARRY, in conversation with JAMA+ AI Editor in Chief Roy Perlis. This podcast was recorded using OpenAI's ChatGPT in voice mode, via web interface, running on GPT-4o. Related Content: What Can 50-Year-Old Chatbots Teach Us About Clinical Applications of AI?

Jan 29

12 min 12 sec
